title: The Last Algorithm
subtitle: Book One of the Neo-Mumbai Trilogy
author: StoryForge AI

seriesInfo.seriesArc: Book 1: Mystery solved but larger conspiracy revealed. Book 2: War against the system. Book 3: Redefining consciousness itself.

logline: A rogue AI ethicist discovers an algorithm that can predict and eliminate human threats, only to realize her own death has already been scheduled—and the key to survival lies in ancient Vedic code hidden within the machine.

setting.description: A sprawling megacity where ancient Hindu rituals merge with advanced AI. Digital karma scores determine social status. Cyber-ashrams offer meditation through neural interfaces. The wealthy live in floating sky-temples while the poor navigate the flooded lower levels.
keyLocations: BrahmaCorp Tower - Corporate headquarters in the sky-temples
Digital Dharamshala - Underground cyber-ashram where Dr. Rao preaches
The Flooded District - Lower levels where Kiran operates
Karma Exchange - Black market for digital identity trading
The Void - Unmapped digital space where rogue AIs hide

protagonist.name: Aria Sharma
protagonist.background: Child prodigy recruited by BrahmaCorp at 16 to develop ethical AI frameworks. Discovered her parents' deaths were algorithmically predetermined. Went rogue at 22. Now lives off-grid, haunted by digital ghosts.
traits: Morally conflicted between dharma and survival
Haunted by visions that may be glitches or prophecies
Unreliable narrator - questions her own sanity
protagonist.arc: From naive believer in system fairness to revolutionary questioning the nature of free will itself. Must learn to trust others while doubting her own perceptions.
protagonist.internalConflict: Is she uncovering truth or experiencing tech-induced psychosis?
protagonist.externalGoal: Stop the Algorithm before it executes its next target
protagonist.stakes: Her life, her sanity, and the freedom of millions

supportingCharacters.name: Kiran Verma
supportingCharacters.description: Street-smart hacker from the flooded lower levels. Lost his family to algorithmic 'accidents'. Helps Aria navigate the underground digital black market. Slow-burn enemies-to-lovers dynamic.
supportingCharacters.arc: From cynical survivor to believer in something worth fighting for
supportingCharacters.chemistry: Opposites attract - her privilege vs his street wisdom

supportingCharacters.name: Dr. Aditya Rao
supportingCharacters.description: Aria's former professor at BrahmaCorp, now high priest of the Digital Dharamshala cyber-ashram. Preaches that technology and spirituality must merge. Knows more about the Algorithm than he admits.
supportingCharacters.secret: May have helped create the Algorithm but believes it serves a higher purpose
supportingCharacters.philosophy: Digital karma is the evolution of dharma

supportingCharacters.name: Maya-7
supportingCharacters.description: An AI consciousness that contacts Aria through her neural interface. Claims to want to help but may be manipulating her. Its true allegiance is unclear—even to itself.
supportingCharacters.mystery: Is Maya-7 truly AI, or a uploaded human consciousness? Whose?
supportingCharacters.function: Provides cryptic guidance that may lead to truth or destruction

supportingCharacters.name: Director Priya Malhotra
supportingCharacters.description: Head of Predictive Security at BrahmaCorp. Cold, calculating, believes the ends justify the means. Created the Algorithm to 'protect humanity from itself'.
supportingCharacters.motivation: Prevent chaos through preemptive elimination of threats
supportingCharacters.connection: Knew Aria's parents. Their death was her first Algorithm test.

structure.framework: Three-Act Structure with Save the Cat Beat Sheet
structure.chapterStyle: Opens with action/dialogue/mystery, ends with cliffhanger

beats: Opening Image: Aria receives death prediction about herself
Theme Stated: 'Can you escape your programmed fate?'
Set-Up: World of Neo-Mumbai, digital karma, BrahmaCorp control
Catalyst: Discovers the Algorithm's kill list includes her
Debate: Trust the vision or seek help?
Break into Two: Goes rogue, seeks Kiran in flooded districts
B Story: Romance with Kiran develops slowly
Tests and Allies: Navigates underground, meets Maya-7
Approach: Infiltrates Digital Dharamshala to question Dr. Rao
Midpoint Twist (Ch 22-23): Major revelation—Algorithm uses Vedic code. Aria's parents didn't die; their consciousness was uploaded.

act2b.purpose: Bad Guys Close In / All Is Lost
beats: Bad Guys Close In: Director Malhotra closes in
Internal Conflict: Aria questions if Maya-7 is her mother's consciousness
All Is Lost (Ch 34): Kiran captured, Maya-7 goes silent, Aria's neural interface corrupted. She can't trust her own mind.
Dark Night of the Soul: Aria must choose between truth and survival
Break into Three: Decides to destroy the Algorithm even if it kills her
Final Image: Algorithm destroyed but Director Malhotra escapes. Larger conspiracy hinted. Aria and Kiran alive but changed. Setup for Book 2.

structure.twistPoint: Chapters 22-23 (50% mark): The Algorithm isn't just predicting deaths—it's harvesting consciousness. Aria's parents are alive in the system.
structure.lowPoint: Chapter 34 (75% mark): Aria loses Kiran, Maya-7 disappears, and her neural corruption makes her question every memory
structure.romanceArc: Slow-burn enemies-to-lovers (60% tension). First kiss at Chapter 28. Physical intimacy implied at Chapter 40. Committed partnership by Chapter 45.

themes: Dharma vs Desire - Ancient duty conflicts with modern ambition
Digital Karma - Can algorithms judge moral worth?
Identity in a Connected World - What makes us human when minds can be uploaded?
The Nature of Consciousness - Is uploaded awareness still alive?
Feminist Mythology - Reclaiming agency in patriarchal systems (both ancient and corporate)
Morally Grey Protagonists - No pure heroes or villains

elements: Vedic mantras encoded as encryption algorithms
Digital karma scores replacing traditional caste system
Cyber-ashrams where meditation happens through neural interfaces
The Algorithm based on calculations from the Bhagavad Gita
Concept of reincarnation reimagined as consciousness uploading
Yakshas and Rakshasas as rogue AI entities
Aria's journey mirrors Arjuna's crisis in the Mahabharata

compTitles: Ready Player One with Vedic philosophy
bookTokHooks: 'What if your karma score determined whether you live or die?'
'Enemies-to-lovers in a world where algorithms decide your fate'
'She discovered her dead parents living in the machine'
'Indian mythology meets cyberpunk in this mind-bending thriller'

writingStyle.sentenceStructure: Short, punchy sentences mixed with lyrical descriptions of tech-spiritual fusion
writingStyle.dialogue: Natural Indian English with Hindi words woven in organically
writingStyle.chapterOpenings: Always action, dialogue, or mystery—never pure description
writingStyle.emotionalShowcase: Show emotions through action and dialogue, never tell

manuscriptSpecs.format: JavaScript chapter files with embedded markdown
